stages.title: Corpus
stages.summary: Offline poewiki ingest into a local index.
stages.detail:
Where data comes from: Path of Exile 1 articles on poewiki.net, listed in seed_pages.yaml (18 mechanic pages) for offline ingest.

How we decided: A small allowlist keeps ingest fast and answers focused on core mechanics — not the full ~16k-article wiki.

What runs: poe-ingest writes data/raw/, chunks.jsonl, and a Chroma vector index. With RETRIEVAL_MODE=live (default), Ask can skip the local corpus and fetch pages at query time.

alternatives.label: Full wiki dump
alternatives.detail:
Industry pattern: Mirror the entire wiki (dump or crawl-all) into one search index.

Here: Not implemented — would raise storage, ingest time, and noise. See Scaling to the full wiki in this doc.

alternatives.label: Live fetch per query
alternatives.detail:
Industry pattern: Fetch missing pages at question time (dynamic RAG).

Here: Default via RETRIEVAL_MODE=live — MediaWiki search, fetch, disk cache (data/live_cache/), in-memory chunk + rerank. Set RETRIEVAL_MODE=local to use only the pre-built index.

stages.title: Ask
stages.summary: Your question enters the harness API.
stages.detail:
What happens: The React UI sends your question (and provider choice) to POST /query. No retrieval yet — this step only captures input and settings.

Also: POST /settings/provider, optional POST /transcribe for voice.

alternatives.label: Streamlit UI
alternatives.detail: Earlier prototype UI on port 8501 — removed in favor of React + Vite served from web/dist/.

stages.title: Retrieve
stages.summary: Live poewiki (default) or local hybrid + rerank.
stages.detail:
Modes (RETRIEVAL_MODE):

  • live — multi-query fusion (verbatim user question + variants), title probe, union fetch, cross-encoder rerank vs user question.
  • local — dense Chroma + BM25 RRF on the ingested index.
  • hybrid — local first; live fusion when local scores are weak.

Quality: Title overlap filter; optional RETRIEVAL_REFINE_ENABLED for one gated retry. Trace: retrieval_source, retrieval_refined.

alternatives.label: Dense only
alternatives.detail:
What it is: Semantic vector search only — good for paraphrases, weaker on exact item names.

Here: We always combine dense with BM25.

alternatives.label: Sparse only
alternatives.detail:
What it is: BM25 / keyword search — strong on exact terms, weaker on rephrased questions.

Here: Used together with dense in hybrid mode.

alternatives.label: No rerank
alternatives.detail:
What it is: Take top fusion results without a cross-encoder second pass.

Here: Cross-encoder rerank runs when the model loads successfully.

stages.title: Plan
stages.detail:
LangGraph (not “line graph”) is an optional planner: for some compare-style questions it may issue 1–3 retrieval subtasks before synthesis — e.g. separate searches for ignite vs poison.

Default path: Linear RAG — one retrieve_for_query call with your full question.

Planner runs when retrieval is available (indexed corpus or RETRIEVAL_MODE=live|hybrid) and the provider is not stub; otherwise retrieval stays single-pass.

alternatives.label: Linear only
alternatives.detail:
What it is: Single retrieval pass with the user question — the classic RAG loop.

Here: Always available; used when LangGraph does not plan extra steps.

alternatives.label: Rule-based planner
alternatives.detail:
What it is: Heuristics instead of an LLM to split compare questions.

Here: Fallback when LLM planning fails.

stages.title: Generate
stages.summary: LLM or stub writes from retrieved chunks only.
stages.detail:
What we do: The selected provider (stub, Ollama, Claude, or GPT-4) generates an answer using only the retrieved excerpts — not the whole corpus.

Stub: Returns a wiki excerpt with no LLM call.

LLM modes: Short grounded answer with citations; system prompt enforces PoE 1 and excerpt-only rules.

alternatives.label: Stub
alternatives.detail: No generation model — fastest path, shows top chunk text for debugging retrieval.

alternatives.label: Bedrock
alternatives.detail: AWS Bedrock as a cloud provider — supported via .env for generation or judges, not exposed in the UI toggle.

stages.title: Judge
stages.summary: Retrieval metrics on Evaluate; 3 inline scores on Ask.
stages.detail:
Retrieval (Ask): LLM judges for context precision and context recall on retrieved excerpts (0–100% in UI).

Generation (Ask): faithfulness, relevance, prompt adherence — three more judges.

Evaluate: exact page-title precision/recall when expected_pages are supplied.

Judge model: JUDGE_PROVIDER (default Ollama), separate from the answer provider.

alternatives.label: Skip inline judges
alternatives.detail: Set INLINE_EVAL=false in .env to skip generation judges on /query (faster, no 1–5 scores in UI).

alternatives.label: Gold /evaluate
alternatives.detail: Harness Evaluate button runs retrieval precision/recall plus generation judges and optional extraction vs a gold answer — for regression testing, not casual Q&A.

stages.title: Respond
stages.summary: JSON with citations, timing, and scores.
stages.detail:
What you get: Final answer, wiki citations, quality_scores (three generation metrics), and trace — plan steps, tool calls, chunk previews, per-phase timing, and LLM I/O for learning/debugging.

Scores never rewrite the answer; they are feedback for you and portfolio demos.
